title: geonoma pohliana subsp. linharensis
intro: Geonoma pohliana subsp. linharensis, also known as the Linhares geonoma palm, is a subspecies of palm tree native to the tropical forests of Brazil. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antiviral properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Geonoma pohliana subsp. linharensis are typically used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and digestive problems.
